qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via unrestricted URL in Chromium convert endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding describes SSRF via empty allow/deny list defaults in the Chromium module (pkg/modules/chromium/). Advisory describes arbitrary file write via case-insensitive ExifTool tag bypass in pkg/modules/exiftool/exiftool.go. Different module, different root cause, different vulnerability class, different endpoint.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via downloadFrom form field
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding describes SSRF via empty allow/deny list defaults in the API module (pkg/modules/api/). Advisory describes arbitrary file write via case-insensitive ExifTool tag bypass in pkg/modules/exiftool/exiftool.go. Different module, different root cause, different vulnerability class, different endpoint.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via webhook URL headers
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Finding describes SSRF via empty allow/deny list defaults in the webhook module (pkg/modules/webhook/). Advisory describes arbitrary file write via case-insensitive ExifTool tag bypass in pkg/modules/exiftool/exiftool.go. Different module, different root cause, different vulnerability class, different endpoint.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory (GHSA-qmwh-9m9c-h36m) describes an incomplete fix for ExifTool arbitrary file write in Gotenberg: case-sensitive Go delete() fails to block alternate-casing ExifTool pseudo-tags (e.g., 'filename' bypasses 'FileName'), and the blocklist omits HardLink/SymLink tags. The vulnerable code is in pkg/modules/exiftool/exiftool.go lines 231-237, exploited via the /forms/pdfengines/metadata/write endpoint. None of the three qualifying findings involve the ExifTool module, the metadata write endpoint, case-sensitivity bypass, or ExifTool tag handling. All three findings describe SSRF via empty allow/deny list defaults in unrelated modules (Chromium, API, Webhook). No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause.
evidence: Advisory root cause: case-sensitive Go delete() in pkg/modules/exiftool/exiftool.go:231-237 fails to block alternate-casing ExifTool pseudo-tags
Advisory CWE: CWE-178 (Improper Handling of Case Sensitivity), CWE-73 (External Control of File Name or Path)
Finding ssrf-chromium-url: targets pkg/modules/chromium/ routes, different module and root cause
Finding ssrf-download-from: targets pkg/modules/api/ context.go, different module and root cause
Finding ssrf-webhook: targets pkg/modules/webhook/ middleware.go, different module and root cause
